epic.priority: Derived from the sketch-ink-paint proposal (sketch-ink-paint.proposal.md) and bdo's decisions in chat, 2026-06-19, recorded here so the build inherits them from the record, not from chat (the epic.experience-layer grain). The arc is NOT yet confirmed — bdo confirms it with `loop.node confirm-arc --epic epic.three-marks --by bdo` when ready, and only then does the merge-node land its PRs. Pieces are wave-ordered (§9.3 at epic scale): a wave does not open until the prior wave has a passing receipt. The decisions, verbatim in intent: (1) 'mark' is a fold LABEL first, not doctrine — the one-sentence doctrine extension is promoted only after the label reads true on the live backlog (marks proposed, not minted); (2) sketch governance UNCHANGED — the inker is still the gate (D-2), un-inked sketch is made countable, not differently governed; (3) un-admitted writes are ACCEPTED-AND-STAMPED `mark: sketch` and folded as un-inked pressure, never refused at the seam (refusal strands work the way the merge-node's 22 landings were stranded); (4) FULL epic with a done-line series (this file), bdo's choice over a single done-line; (5) the pentimento/sauce organ graduates to its own arc `epic.pentimento` after waves 1-2. Naming ruling: 'experience' carries THREE incompatible senses to reconcile (causality's overload teeth), named rather than fought over — (a) LIVED experience: the maker's first-person encounter ending in an authored story, already bound by epic.experience-layer; (b) CRAFT / the sauce: memory made comparable, reduced and kept — the pentimento organ, named for its mechanism (pentimento / palimpsest), NOT 'experience'; (c) FELT experience (bdo, 2026-06-19): the felt feel of the work and its media, the anima — the quality of encountering the made thing, not the maker's process — which is the deep muse of the PAINT stage (paint's job is not only legibility but feeling). These are the maker's, the system's, and the work's experience respectively.
epic.arc: ontum built a rich, disciplined PEN — a single sanctioned write path per surface, enforcing carbon-copy determinism, receipt-or-it-didn't-happen, no-self-sign, additive-never-erase. But 'pen' in the craft it is named after is one of three marks: an illustrated board is penciled (provisional, erasable), then inked (committed), then painted over (additive, surfaced). ontum mastered the INK and left the other two unnamed — and almost every recurring flaw lives in that absence: off-log strands are sketch torn out of the book; the merge-node's unadmitted landings are ink drawn by a hand never admitted to hold the pen; mocks are pencil in ink's slot; owner-asks parked in unread reports are paint that never reached the wall. This epic names the two missing marks and enforces the boundary between them, so a flaw becomes a legible 'mark made in the wrong stage' instead of a one-off janitorial gap. Nothing here is a new subsystem: every piece is a label, a read-only fold, or a boundary on an existing pen — the ink machinery already built is the win we keep.
epic.context: The correcting principle (bdo, this session): a pencil sketch belongs on the log too — erased, it still leaves a trace. The first instinct (make sketch cheap by making it off-log) is the SAME wrong that produced the off-log strands: a sketch torn out of the book can be neither inked nor COMPARED, and comparison is the only way the system gets better. ontum already owns the mechanism — the whiteout/supersede leaves a mark you can still see under — so all three marks share one medium, the log: what makes a mark a sketch is not that it lives off-book but that it MAY be erased and the erasure is itself a logged trace, never a deletion (D-5, 'history is never retro-invalidated', applied to the earliest mark). The pentimento runs across all three marks (the trace the eraser leaves; the log accumulated is a palimpsest; the lore distilled is craft) and is the reason any of this is worth doing — but it graduates to epic.pentimento and is out of THIS epic's remit. Evidence base for the diagnosis: loop.gaps, loop.census, and reports 0042/0043/0057/0059/0062/0065/0068. The destructiveness principle (bdo, 2026-06-19): pen and paint can RUIN work if applied incorrectly — ink and paint are permanent (an append-only log mark can be painted over via supersede but never erased, D-5), so the cost of a wrong commit is damage, not a gap. This is why this epic's boundaries are strict and why decision (3) accepts-and-stamps rather than inks-by-default: the doctrine's guards (D-2 no-self-sign, write-done-before-start, cooling/I-7) are protections against ruining the board by committing in permanent media at sketch-stage confidence. Restraint — knowing when to WITHHOLD the pen, leaving a line in pencil until it earns ink — is the capability ontum is shortest on, and over-inking (mocks-as-real, premature merges) is its measured pathology.
epic.value: The system stops leaking work at the edges and stops mistaking one stage's method for another's. A waking session inherits a stage-aware situation (un-inked sketch / leaking ink / paint on the wrong wall) instead of a janitorial gap, and the owner inherits the same colored for him: the un-inked is the agents' work, the unreadable paint is his to receive, one fence-stroke unblocks the colorist. The deepest payoff is deferred to epic.pentimento, but this epic is its precondition: you cannot keep a sauce the kitchen never let simmer — the sketchbook (waves 1-2) is what gives the palimpsest fold something comparable to read.
epic.horizon: Three marks legible on the live backlog. loop.gaps labels every gap with its mark (sketch/ink/paint) and a fixture test proves the labels read true against the real backlog (wave 1). A cheap sketch primitive lands draft marks on the log (paired with the existing whiteout eraser), and an `un_inked()` fold counts sketch pretending to be truth — drafts with no atom, effective-mocks, proposals with no done-line — handing the count to every waking session via one summon line (wave 1). The records/node pens consult `effective_mocks()` at write time: an un-admitted actor's write is accepted but stamped `mark: sketch` and folds as un-inked pressure until `admit-real` inks the actor, closing the merge-node's 22-landing leak structurally (wave 2). The reflect/auto beat enforces paint's medium: a paint act is 'delivered' only when it lands on a registered reader surface, and an ask resolving only to a report file folds as paint-on-the-wrong-medium (wave 3). The owner-gated colorist stroke — the slow-loop write-back inside bdo's drawn auto_admit_fence — lays the dial correction (wave 4, inert until he draws the fence). Done when the doctrine carries the one mark-sentence (promoted because the label proved true), and the pentimento organ has graduated to epic.pentimento with a sketchbook to read.

pieces.status: wave 1 — the cheapest proof the framework reads true
pieces.glue: Step 1. A `mark` label on the existing folds' vocabulary, not new code. loop.gaps already separates mock/unadmitted (sketch) from parked-piece (ink-in-waiting) from surface-drift (paint-leak); have it LABEL each gap with its mark so the summon line says 'this is un-inked sketch' / 'this is paint that never reached the wall'. Pure relabel of an existing read-only fold — no write path touched. The §10 test with teeth: a fixture backlog where the label would mis-mark a gap must fail. This is decision (1) made concrete — the label proves itself before any doctrine sentence is minted.

pieces.status: wave 1 — the sketchbook back in the book (bdo: the first increment)
pieces.glue: Step 2. The ONE new write path this epic adds: a cheap sketch primitive that lands a draft mark on the log (paired with the existing whiteout as its eraser), so exploration and abandonment both leave traces and nothing falls out of the book. Then one read-only fold `un_inked()` = sketch marks with no atom behind them + effective-mocks + proposals with no done-line, ranked — the off-log gate (PR #123) generalized from PRs to all three sketch media — surfaced as one summon line every waking session gets. The §10 case: the 50 stranded commits + 14 Causality increments must read as countable, comparable sketch, not a silent strand. Carries decision (2): sketch becomes countable, not differently governed.

pieces.status: wave 2 — does not open until wave 1 has a passing receipt
pieces.glue: Step 3, carrying decision (3) verbatim: ACCEPT-AND-STAMP, never refuse at the seam. Make the records/node pens consult `effective_mocks()` at write time — a write by an un-admitted actor is accepted but stamped `mark: sketch` (never lost, never silently promoted to ink) and folds as un-inked pressure until an `admit-real` inks the actor. This closes the merge-node's 22-landing leak structurally instead of by shaming. The §10 test: an un-admitted actor's landing must surface as stamped sketch pressure, and a hard-refusal variant (the rejected fork) must be shown to strand the same work — proving why accept-and-stamp was chosen.

pieces.status: wave 3 — does not open until wave 2 has a passing receipt
pieces.glue: Step 4. The paint-medium contract on the reflect/auto beat: a paint act (owner-ask, divergence, digest line) is 'delivered' only when it lands on a registered reader surface (loop.reflect's surface registry). An ask that resolves only to a report file folds as paint-on-the-wrong-medium and is surfaced — the owner-ask-shame beat made a first-class paint-stage check rather than a bolt-on. The §10 case: an owner-ask written only into a working-tree report must fold as undelivered paint (exactly the failure screaming on the log this session).

pieces.status: wave 4 — owner-gated; inert until bdo draws the fence
pieces.glue: Step 5. The colorist's stroke: the slow-loop write-back, the colorist laying the dial correction INSIDE bdo's drawn auto_admit_fence (loop.disposer). The disposer's auto_admit_fence already exists; this wires the write-back so an in-fence proposal self-admits citing the fence as authorized_by (the loop executes bdo's standing stamp, never signs its own line — the confirm-arc/merge-node shape, one layer down, D-4). Inert until bdo draws the fence — the right shape: the authorization is his, the stroke is the loop's.

pieces.status: graduates to epic.pentimento after waves 1-2 (decision 5) — named here so the arc reads whole, NOT to double-build
pieces.glue: Step 6 lives in its own arc. The palimpsest fold — a read over the log's SUPERSEDED and ABANDONED layers (not its current surface) whose output is craft the next session inherits at wake, the way it inherits a gap today. It is the organ that turns memory + meaning into experience (the sauce: a kept, fed reduction, the starter being the palimpsest and the sauce the fold over it). It is the system's deepest gap (every fold today reads the log down to current state and throws the journey away; the slow loop cannot even write back its one dial — report 0062). It does NOT belong here: it earns epic.pentimento once waves 1-2 give it a sketchbook to read — you cannot fold a journey the book never recorded. Naming reserves 'pentimento/palimpsest' for its mechanism, leaving 'experience' to be reconciled against epic.experience-layer's prior, different binding of the word (the overload causality would flag).
